title: Can I 'lookup' multiple items in a connected board?
excerpt:
This seems like a basic relational database function but I’m new to Monday and can’t figure out how to do this: 
I’m tracking Authors and Blog Posts from our website. On the Blog Posts board, I have a Connect column to t…

posts.author: daniellerch (Daniel Lerch)
posts.cookedHtml:
This seems like a basic relational database function but I’m new to Monday and can’t figure out how to do this:

I’m tracking Authors and Blog Posts from our website. On the Blog Posts board, I have a Connect column to the Author board – so, for example, Blog Post board item “Ice cream is good” is connected to Author board item “Ben Cohen”. Ben has written a few articles, and there are 3 different Blog Post board items assocaited with him.

What I’d like to be able to do is go Author board item “Ben Cohen” and be able to see all the Blog Post board items assocaited with him. What do I do?

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: Which product (CRM/Work Management, etc) and level (Pro/Enterprise) do you have?

posts.author: daniellerch (Daniel Lerch)
posts.cookedHtml: I have these boards in a “CRM” workspace, and we are on the Nonprofit Pro Plan.

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
I threw this together, let me know if it helps?

Desiree - www.thecleverclovers.com

posts.author: daniellerch (Daniel Lerch)
posts.cookedHtml: Perfect, this is exactly what I needed to know. Thanks, Desiree!
